results.title: Administrative Data Used to Compile Wealth Data in Occasional Paper of 9 February 2026
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance directed that the query regarding the list of administrative data used to compile wealth data, the inclusion of overseas assets in household income calculations, and the specific values for each decile broken down by local versus overseas contributions be answered in the response to Questions Nos 1 and 2 during the Parliament Sitting on 25 February 2026. No new details or specific figures were provided in this session. The full breakdown of wealth data sources and overseas asset values remains pending the referenced parliamentary answer.

results.title: Adequacy of Child-minding Services to Support Mothers Working on Shifts
results.summary: The Ministry removed the requirement for childcare centres to operate on Saturdays effective January 2025, as surveys indicated over 99% of families did not need Saturday services or could arrange alternatives. For parents requiring weekend support, the Ministry points to existing paid and community-based options like babysitting services. Additionally, employers are encouraged to provide family-friendly environments under the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests, which took effect on 1 December 2024 to help workers manage caregiving needs. The Minister noted that less than 1% of surveyed families had both parents working on Saturdays without alternative arrangements. No new formal childcare resources specifically for shift workers were announced during the debate.

results.title: 25th Percentile, Median and 75th Percentile Income of Top 1,000 Earners Who Are Singapore Citizens
results.summary: For the Year of Assessment 2024, the 25th, median, and 75th percentile incomes of the top 1,000 Singapore citizen earners are $2.5 million, $3.1 million, and $4.5 million respectively. These figures were provided by the Minister for Finance in response to a parliamentary question regarding high-income earner statistics. The data reflects the specific income distribution for this cohort within the stated assessment year.

results.title: Actions Against Companies who Pay CPF Contributions to Phantom Local Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower detects phantom worker schemes using data analytics, inspections, and complaint investigations. Companies inflating foreign worker quotas via CPF contributions to non-employees face financial penalties of up to $20,000 per affected work pass application and will be debarred from hiring foreign workers. Colluding phantom workers may also face enforcement actions for abetting the offence.

results.title: Allowing Private Healthcare Providers Access to Medications Not Found on Healthier SG Medication List
results.summary: The Ministry of Health will not allow private healthcare providers to procure drugs outside the Healthier SG Medication List from the Agency of Logistics and Procurement Services (ALPS). The Minister stated this restriction prevents a centralised national procurement strategy that could negatively impact Singapore's small market. Consequently, such a move might result in less favourable prices for subsidised patients, reduce incentives for pharmaceutical companies to enter the market, and limit the overall range of available drugs. No implementation timeline is applicable as the plan is not proceeding.

results.title: Assessing Trend of Declining Job Creation Per Dollar of Investment
results.summary: The Ministry assesses that declining job creation per dollar of investment will persist due to a challenging global environment and technological shifts reducing reliance on traditional hiring. While the Singapore Economic Development Board secured investment commitments comparable to recent years in 2025, the lower job count reflects conservative corporate hiring projections and a shift toward technology- and capital-intensive projects that require fewer workers but generate higher value-add. Of the 15,700 jobs expected over the next five years, most are professional, manager, executive, or technician roles, with two-thirds projected to command a gross monthly salary above S$5,000. The Ministry does not provide a breakdown of entry-level versus senior management roles, noting that these investments generate positive spillovers including higher local employment and wages for firms linked to EDB-supported companies.

results.title: Breakdown of Enhanced Matched Retirement Savings Scheme Take-Up Rate by Demographics and Source of Top-ups
results.summary: Since January 2025, the Enhanced Matched Retirement Savings Scheme removed the age cap of 70 and increased the annual matching grant from $600 to $2,000, with a $20,000 lifetime limit. These changes drove grant recipients from approximately 103,000 in 2024 to 250,000 in 2025. Demographically, 55% of 2025 recipients were aged above 70, compared to 0% in 2024, while female participation remained stable at roughly 62-65%. Regarding top-up sources, 52% of recipients contributed personally in 2025, slightly down from 53% in 2024, with loved ones providing 46% of top-ups versus 44% previously.

results.title: Clarification by Leader of the House
results.summary: Leader of the House Ms Indranee Rajah issued a factual correction to her reply from 27 February 2026 regarding grassroots adviser roles. She clarified that these roles flow from the Government, distinct from the role of an MP, referencing prior explanations by Minister Ong Ye Kung. The Leader stated she will not address the matter in detail beyond this correction. No new policy measures, timelines, or eligibility rules were introduced in this specific intervention.

results.title: Clarification by Minister of State for Home Affairs
results.summary: Minister of State for Home Affairs Mr Goh Pei Ming issued a factual correction regarding Government of India (GOIS) scam statistics for 2025. The corrected statement confirms that reported cases more than doubled to over 3,000, placing GOIS among the top five scam types. While it remains the scam type with the highest average loss per case, the average amount is now stated as more than $72,000, representing the second highest average loss compared to other scam types. This clarification updates the previous parliamentary record from 27 February 2026.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for Health
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for Health Dr Koh Poh Koon clarified that resources for outcome studies on public health interventions were initiated in recent years, not approximately one year ago as previously stated. He confirmed that data collection and impact analysis will occur over time to gain better insights for enhancing these interventions. The Minister also issued a factual correction to his remarks made during Question Time on 27 February 2026, updating the timeline for the commencement of these studies.

results.title: Collaboration Guidelines between Social Service Agencies and Town Councils for Community Outreach Awareness
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development clarified that while Social Service Agencies may collaborate with Town Councils on estate management issues, they cannot engage in outreach, community engagement, or service delivery. These activities fall outside the Town Councils' mandate, which is strictly limited to controlling, managing, maintaining, and improving common property in Housing and Development Board housing estates. Consequently, no guidelines exist permitting such collaborations for community awareness initiatives, as these functions remain prohibited under current operational constraints.

results.title: Collecting Disability Employment Data Disaggregated by Disability Type
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not currently collect or publish disability employment data disaggregated by specific disability types, such as intellectual disabilities or autism spectrum disorder. The Minister explained that further disaggregation would introduce wide margins of error and significant year-to-year fluctuations due to the small proportion of persons with disabilities in the total population. Instead, MOM relies on feedback from stakeholders via the Ministry of Social and Family Development and SG Enable to inform policy reviews. Additionally, the Taskforce on Assurance for Families with Persons with Disabilities is developing recommendations to enhance support for persons with disabilities and employers in the evolving employment landscape.

results.title: Committee of Supply Reporting Progress
results.summary: The Committee of Supply reported progress on the Estimates of Expenditure for the Financial Year 2026/2027. Senior Parliamentary Secretary Eric Chua moved to report this progress and requested leave to sit again on Monday, 2 March 2026. The Deputy Speaker granted the motion, allowing the committee to resume proceedings on the specified date. No further details regarding specific expenditure items or budget figures were provided in this report.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head F (Parliament)
results.summary: MP He Ting Ru moved to reduce Head F (Parliament) spending by $100, arguing that strengthening parliamentary democracy requires clear distinctions between partisan and non-partisan roles. She called for cooling-off periods for public servants and political appointees transitioning between roles to prevent conflicts of interest, citing international precedents and the need to maintain public trust. She also questioned the partisan nature of grassroots advisors under the People's Association, urging clarity on whether they function as non-partisan state extensions or party machinery. Minister Indranee Rajah clarified that while a formal cooling-off period for MPs is not required, transparency is maintained by declaring role switches; she noted that grassroots advisor roles are not a parliamentary matter and referred to previous explanations by Minister Ong Ye Kung.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head R (Ministry of Law)
results.summary: The Ministry of Law is studying enhancements to the civil enforcement process, including granting Courts greater powers to identify assets of judgment debtors and introducing Civil Judgment Enforcement Officers to address low utilisation of tribunals due to digital and financial barriers. Concurrently, the Ministry is examining means-testing thresholds for civil legal aid, specifically for probate and deputyship applications, to improve accessibility for lower-income Singaporeans amidst cost-of-living pressures. In parallel, initiatives to strengthen Singapore's role as a global legal hub include developing ESG capabilities, supporting in-house legal counsels in AI adoption, and safeguarding human creative contributions against GenAI training concerns. The Ministry also confirmed it is reviewing the Land Betterment Charge regime and exploring the use of state land for sports and recreation to enhance community infrastructure.

results.title: Compatibility Issues between ERP 2.0 On-board Units and Some Car Park Gantry Systems
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that all ERP 2.0 On-Board Units are fully compatible with existing Electronic Parking System carparks. Reported access issues stem from antenna range limitations rather than system incompatibility, a constraint also present in the ERP 1 system. Affected motorists can resolve these problems by repositioning their vehicle or recalibrating the OBU antenna. No specific incident count or new interoperability steps were reported beyond these remedial actions.

results.title: Considerations Behind Government's Recent Announcement of UNC3886 Cyberattacks
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information stated that the Government publicly announced UNC3886 cyberattacks targeting Singapore's telecommunications sector, a critical infrastructure providing essential services. While not all attacks are disclosed, the announcement aims to ensure public awareness of real threats and the need for vigilance. The Government emphasized that adequate investment in cybersecurity is required, with the public and private sectors collaborating to build resilience against sophisticated, persistent cyber threats and potential disruptions to essential services.

results.title: Data for Malays Granted Permanent Residency and Citizenship
results.summary: The Minister stated that data on Permanent Residency and citizenship grants by nationality, ethnicity, or religion is not published. While the current ethnic mix as of June 2025 is available, specific annual figures for Malays are unavailable. The government explained that immigration is necessary to counteract low birth rates and support national security, defence, and economic dynamism. Consequently, the approach to immigration remains careful and measured while prioritizing efforts to encourage Singaporeans to start families.

results.title: Data on Enforcement Challenges Faced by Traffic Police Related to Non-standard Number Plates in Last Three Years
results.summary: The Traffic Police has encountered challenges identifying vehicles with non-standard registration plates, such as reflective or small plates, which violate the Road Traffic (Motor Vehicles, Registration and Licensing) Rules. Upon encountering such cases, the Traffic Police refers them to the Land Transport Authority for enforcement. While the use of non-conforming plates is an offence, the authorities do not track the specific number of instances occurring in the last three years.

results.title: Data on Formula One Singapore Grand Prix Cess Collection, Incurred Expenses and Contribution to GDP
results.summary: For the 2022 to 2025 Formula One Singapore Grand Prix editions, an average of $30 million in cess has been collected against estimated annual government costs of $140 million. Since 2008, the event has generated over $2.2 billion in incremental tourism receipts. The Government does not assess individual tourism events for their specific contribution to national gross domestic product.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head J (Ministry of Defence)
results.summary: Ministry of Defence (MINDEF) reaffirms that National Service remains the bedrock of Singapore's defence, with allowances not pegged to the Local Qualifying Salary but serving as a civic duty. To address evolving threats including unmanned systems and cyber risks, MINDEF will integrate sensors and digital networks while diversifying supply chains and expanding volunteerism to women, new citizens, and PRs through SAFRA and community institutions. The Ministry plans to transform SAFRA into a national defence movement to foster inter-generational understanding and track outcomes for community-anchored Total Defence. While specific threat scenarios and operational details are withheld to protect security, MINDEF commits to long-term fiscal sustainability, adapting capabilities over 10–20 years to maintain Singapore's value proposition as a trusted, neutral convener amidst great power competition.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head P (Ministry of Home Affairs)
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) outlined a multi-pronged strategy to counter domestic threats, including terrorism, cyberattacks, and scams, emphasizing anticipatory measures and strong stakeholder partnerships. To address evolving digital risks, the Government is leveraging smart technologies via the 2024 AI Movement ($400M budget) and has established a dedicated cryptocurrency tracing team to combat money-laundering conduits. On ex-offender rehabilitation, the "Supervision 2.0" regime is fully implemented to reduce reporting frequency, while structured aftercare and employer partnerships aim to lower five-year recidivism rates. Regarding drug policy, the approach maintains zero tolerance for traffickers while supporting rehabilitation for addicts through upstream preventive education integrated into school curricula. Additionally, road safety enforcement will tighten with a lowered drink-driving limit and potential demerit point adjustments, alongside high-rise fire safety compliance measures.

results.title: Data on Individuals Granted Permanent Residency under Global Investor Programme
results.summary: From 2015 to 2025, approximately 450 individuals received Permanent Resident status under the Global Investor Programme (GIP), with investments totaling around $930 million across three options: $500 million in direct business investments (mostly in Professional Services, Info-Communications, and Financial Services) and $430 million in GIP-select funds. Eligible investors must commit at least $10 million to a business, $25 million to a fund, or $200 million to a Single Family Office with $50 million in Economic Development Board-specified investments. The Ministry of Trade and Industry does not currently hold data on citizenship conversion rates, PR retention, or re-entry permit lapses, inviting further Parliamentary Questions for those figures. Regarding 2023 scheme changes, the Ministry notes no major shift in applicant profiles yet, as many select fund applicants are still finalizing their investments.

results.title: Data on Prevalence of Mental Health and Neurodevelopmental Conditions in Children under 18 and Treatment Provided in Last Five Years
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, public hospital data shows unique counts of school-going children under 18 diagnosed with mental health or neurodevelopmental conditions, though incidence rates remain unavailable as the Ministry of Health does not track private sector cases. Approximately half of these diagnosed cases in public hospitals receive medication for their conditions. The Minister noted that the provided figures include both newly diagnosed and existing cases seen annually at public facilities.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head N (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs asserts that the post-WWII international order has effectively ended, necessitating a shift from balancing major powers to actively safeguarding Singapore's sovereignty, social cohesion, and prosperity as core national interests. To navigate this fragmented geopolitical landscape, Singapore will reject binary alignments between the US and China, instead pursuing diversified partnerships with immediate neighbours, middle powers, and emerging markets while maintaining strategic autonomy. The government will deepen ASEAN integration ahead of its 2027 chairmanship, leverage technology as soft power, and establish new resident missions to expand diplomatic reach. Furthermore, the MFA will continue humanitarian contributions, such as support for Gaza, and utilise sports and arts for diplomatic outreach, while conducting internal assessments of public engagement sessions without turning strategic foreign policy decisions into public referendums.

results.title: Data on Reported Workplace Heat-related Illnesses
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed the Member to refer to the reply to Question No 18 for Oral Answer at the 26 February 2026 Sitting for data on reported workplace heat-related illnesses. This prior response covers statistics for the past five years, including occurrences in outdoor and indoor settings, as well as outcomes such as hospitalisation, medical leave exceeding three days, and fatalities. The current transcript does not contain the specific numerical data requested.

results.title: Data on Unauthorised CPF Contributions to Non-employees in Past Three Years
results.summary: Over the past three years, the CPF Board received an average of approximately 2,000 annual reports concerning unauthorised contributions to non-employees. These cases are referred to relevant agencies for investigation, specifically targeting potential circumvention of foreign worker quotas and Government support schemes. Subsequent enforcement actions are taken as appropriate based on the investigation outcomes. The Minister did not disclose the total number of detected cases or specific enforcement statistics beyond the volume of reports received.

results.title: Data on Various Payouts and Income Received by Each Decile of Resident Households When Determining Households’ Market Income in 2025
results.summary: The Department of Statistics publishes household market income data for 2025, breaking down non-employment income by decile into investment income, rental income, CPF Retirement Sum Scheme payouts, and Lifelong Income for the Elderly payouts. Regular Government transfers are also included in the published figures. Other non-employment income categories, such as pensions, annuities, royalties, and insurance payouts, are not published separately because their amounts are smaller and subject to larger sampling errors. Consequently, the official dataset aggregates these minor components rather than providing distinct values for each.

results.title: Effect of Extreme Weather Events on Workplace Safety for Those in High-exposure Sectors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) cites the January 2024 Third National Climate Change Study, which projects Singapore's average daily mean temperature rising from 27.9°C to 28.5°C–30.1°C by 2050 alongside intensified rainfall. High-exposure sectors like construction and shipyards face elevated heat stress risks, while flooding threatens equipment safety in various locations. MOM mandates hourly rest breaks of at least 10 minutes for outdoor heavy physical work when the Wet Bulb Globe Temperature reaches 32°C or higher. Employers must report work-related heat illnesses under the Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Act and adhere to MOM and WSH Council guidelines for evaluating weather risks and developing response plans. Enforcement actions are taken against non-compliant companies, with ongoing monitoring of evolving climate risks.

results.title: Eligibility Criteria and Utilisations Rates of Extended Developmental Leave Schemes for Teachers over Past Three Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Education supports teacher professional growth through the Teacher Work Attachment Plus programme, launched in 2021, allowing up to four weeks of industry attachment for over 730 participants. Additionally, professional development leave (PDL) offers up to 12 months' half-pay or 10 weeks' full-pay leave based on years of service, with no-pay options available for longer durations. Over the past three years, more than 360 teachers have utilized these leave schemes. The Minister clarified that while extended developmental leave schemes were considered in 2021, the current framework relies on these existing attachment and leave provisions rather than a new dedicated extended leave scheme.

results.title: Enforcement Mechanisms to Monitor Compliance of Foreign-registered Vehicles Entering Singapore
results.summary: Foreign-registered vehicles with outstanding fines exceeding an undisclosed threshold may be denied entry at Singapore land checkpoints until fines are paid. The Traffic Police also issue a Police Gazette against individuals failing to comply with court summonses for traffic offences. These individuals may be stopped at checkpoints or during Police checks. Specific tools outside joint operations for monitoring compliance are not detailed, and the exact fine threshold remains undisclosed for operational reasons.

results.title: Extending Subsidy and Permission to Use MediSave to Patients with Polyclinic Referrals to Hospitals' Emergency Departments
results.summary: The $50 GPFirst subsidy applies only to GP referrals to Emergency Departments, not polyclinic referrals, as polyclinic evening consultations are unavailable and fees are already heavily subsidised. MediSave usage for non-admitted polyclinic ED referrals remains restricted because the scheme primarily covers hospitalisation, day surgery, MediShield Life, and health insurance premiums, with limited outpatient flexibilities. The Ministry maintains that polyclinic patients do not require the same subsidy offset as GPFirst patients due to existing fee structures and programme design. No extension of these specific benefits to polyclinic referrals was proposed during the debate.

results.title: Extension of Enhanced MRSS for Vulnerable Workers with Career Interruptions Due to Caregiving Reasons
results.summary: The Government will not introduce a new matching scheme but notes that vulnerable workers with career interruptions due to caregiving or medical reasons already benefit from the enhanced Matched Retirement Savings Scheme (MRSS) from age 55, effective 2025. Additional support includes the Silver Support Scheme, Retirement Savings Bonus under the Majulah Package, and the Budget 2026 CPF top-up. Younger workers returning to work may access Workforce Singapore and NTUC EIE employment facilitation services and qualify for the Workfare Income Supplement. Tripartite partners are promoting flexible work arrangements to accommodate those needing flexibility.

results.title: Feasibility Studies for Transit Priority Corridors Connecting Central Area to Towns along North East Line
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed there are no plans for a continuous Transit Priority Corridor along the North East Line connecting the Central Area to towns like Punggol, Sengkang, Hougang, and Serangoon due to insufficient road space. Instead, the Land Transport Authority is implementing alternative features where feasible, including continuous bus lanes, wider footpaths, and dedicated or shared cycling paths. These measures aim to enhance mobility without establishing a full corridor. The Ministry will not conduct further feasibility studies for the continuous corridor under current constraints.

results.title: Feasibility Study of Establishing Domestic Rare Earth Processing Capabilities
results.summary: The Ministry has not yet assessed the feasibility of establishing domestic rare earth processing capabilities but welcomes industry interest in such projects. Singapore adopts a multi-pronged strategy to secure supply chain resilience, which includes supporting a rules-based international trading system, deepening bilateral partnerships, and encouraging firms to diversify critical supply sources. Local production may be considered where economically viable, subject to competing national priorities and resource constraints. Any heavy industrial activity involving radioactive waste must strictly comply with the Radiation Protection Act and Environmental Protection and Management Act. The Ministry is monitoring global supply developments without immediate industry concerns raised.

results.title: Further Professionalising Bus Captain Roles to Attract Younger Singaporeans
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority collaborates with operators and the National Transport Workers' Union to review career progression pathways, enabling bus captains to advance into training, mentorship, or management roles. To attract younger locals, operators offer sign-on bonuses up to $20,000, improved rest facilities, optimized duty rosters, and flexible part-time arrangements. The Government is also reviewing starting salaries to enhance recruitment. Structured training by the Singapore Bus Academy ensures a common skill set for all captains.

results.title: Help for SMEs Heavily Reliant on Point-to-point Travel to Transport Workers Safely
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the Ministry regularly reviews safety measures for workers transported on lorries in consultation with industry stakeholders. Specialist trades using smaller vehicles for point-to-point travel are currently under study to identify options that enhance worker safety while minimizing operational disruption. No new specific measures or implementation timelines were announced at this stage. The Ministry is actively considering solutions to mitigate cost pressures and ensure safe transport for SMEs reliant on lorry deck transport for both workers and equipment.

results.title: Holding Bicycle-sharing Operators Accountable for Bicycles Parked Outside Designated Zones
results.summary: In 2025, 95% of shared bicycle trips ended at designated parking areas. Operators must integrate the Land Transport Authority's QR code parking system, remove improperly parked bicycles within a stipulated timeframe, and impose a $5 fee on users who fail to park correctly. Repeat offenders face fleet reductions, licence suspension, or revocation. Non-compliant operators may incur financial penalties of up to $100,000 or 10% of their annual turnover, whichever is higher. Licence renewal and fleet expansion applications are assessed based on operators' track records in managing indiscriminate parking.

results.title: Implementation of Daily or Monthly Transport Fare Caps for Adult Commuters
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that fare regulation falls under the Public Transport Council (PTC). The PTC is currently evaluating the introduction of daily or monthly fare caps for adult commuters. However, any new fare products must balance extending discounts to specific groups against the additional costs borne by other fare-paying commuters, as concession schemes are generally cross-subsidised. Consequently, the PTC will carefully consider these new fare products to ensure financial sustainability before implementation.

results.title: Integrating p-tau217 Early Detection Blood Tests in National Dementia Strategy
results.summary: The Ministry of Health states that p-tau217 remains under evaluation in local research trials and is not approved as a standalone screening or diagnostic test by the US FDA. Consequently, the Ministry will continue to monitor developments in this field without confirming cost-effectiveness assessments, estimating the eligible population, or including the test in the national dementia strategy at this time. No immediate subsidy or screening inclusion is planned pending further evidence.

results.title: Legal Protections for Individuals Reporting Security Vulnerabilities in Government-contracted Systems
results.summary: The Government does not provide civil or criminal liability exemptions within the Vulnerability Disclosure Programme (VDP) to prevent legitimising intrusive testing, disrupting services, or compromising sensitive data. Instead, a calibrated approach is adopted where general public reports are generally viewed in good faith, while structured programmes like the Government Bug Bounty Programme and Vulnerability Rewards Programme define clear scopes for authorised access and permissions. This strategy aims to benefit from responsible disclosure by the cybersecurity community while safeguarding public systems and maintaining strict boundaries on permissible testing degrees. Consequently, the Ministry will not implement a separate bug bounty programme with legal protections distinct from these existing structured frameworks.

results.title: Making Singapore Workplaces Happier
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) addresses workplace happiness by launching Career Health SG and the Singapore Opportunity Index to support career progression and meaningful growth. Workers can access upskilling programmes like the SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme, while statutory retirement and re-employment ages increase to 64 and 69 respectively by 2026. To foster well-being, MOM introduced the free iWorkHealth tool for psychosocial risk assessment and published a handbook on supporting mental health. The 2025 Workplace Fairness Act, implemented by end-2027, strengthens protections against discrimination based on age, nationality, sex, marital status, pregnancy, caregiving, race, religion, language ability, disability, and mental health conditions. A Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment promotes age-friendly jobs, and MOM partners with the Workplace Safety and Health Council to build employer competencies in mental health support.

results.title: Manpower-related Appeal Cases for Past Two Years Closed Due to Unanswered Callbacks and Alternative Follow-up Procedures
results.summary: In the past two years, approximately 1% of the roughly 500,000 annual appeals received by the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) involved unsuccessful call-backs due to non-response. Before closing such cases, MOM attempts alternative follow-up channels, including email replies and hardcopy letters sent to the appellant's address. The Minister confirmed these procedures are standard practice for handling manpower-related appeal cases where initial contact attempts fail.

results.title: Monitoring Market Concentration by Well-capitalised Foreign Retail Brands in Singapore
results.summary: The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore monitors market concentration risks but currently finds no indication of anti-competitive concerns in the local retail market. To support local retailers against displacement by foreign brands, the Government offers financial schemes including the Enterprise Development Grant, Productivity Solutions Grant, and SG60 vouchers, which have facilitated $2.127 billion in spending at heartland merchants over four years. Specific initiatives like the Enhanced Visual Merchandising Programme, Retail Maverick Challenge, and Design Orchard Retail Singapore provide targeted assistance for storefront upgrades, innovative retail concepts, and premium exposure for over 80 local design brands. The Government continues to collaborate with trade associations to strengthen local capabilities and ensure a diverse, competitive marketplace.

results.title: Noise-related Feedback and Complaints Lodged for Construction Works Near Loyang Viaduct and Cross Island Line Stations
results.summary: Approximately 90 noise-related feedback cases were logged in 2023, rising to about 230 annually in 2024 and 2025, stemming from construction at the Loyang Viaduct and Cross Island Line stations at Loyang and Pasir Ris East. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) directs contractors to engage residents and implement mitigation measures when noise measurements exceed acceptable levels, with severe cases resulting in contractors being taken to task. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these figures and the LTA's enforcement approach but did not provide specific data on the number of cases resolved.

results.title: POHA Cases Against Commuters for Alleged Abuse of Public Transport Workers
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, an average of about 60 cases annually under the Protection from Harassment Act were reported where public transport workers were harassed by commuters. The Government does not track specific instances involving online harassment allegations. Under the Act, using indecent, threatening, abusive, or insulting words or behaviour towards a public transport worker is an offence. Convicted offenders may face fines and/or imprisonment. The Land Transport Authority and operators collaborate with the Singapore Police Force to investigate incidents, while public education efforts continue to promote respect for transport workers.

results.title: Participation Rates of Employment Preparation Scheme and Recidivism Outcomes
results.summary: Between 2023 and 2025, an average of 159 inmates (approximately 4% of the penal population) were annually emplaced on the Employment Preparation Scheme (EmPS), targeting those with poor family support and complex reintegration needs. Eligibility is denied to inmates exhibiting poor conduct, poor response to rehabilitation programmes, or those assessed as a public safety risk. Data indicates a two-year recidivism rate comparison between EmPS participants and all penal inmates, though specific percentage figures for these rates were not provided in the statement. The Minister did not disclose the exact number of inmates emplaced as of January 2026 or the specific proportion for that date.

results.title: Plans to Issue New Banknote Series with Updated Safety and Anti-counterfeiting Features
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance stated that the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has assessed the existing security features of the current Portrait series banknotes and found them effective against prevailing counterfeiting techniques. Although new security features exist, MAS determined that an immediate issuance of a new series is not required as the current notes remain robust. MAS continues to monitor counterfeit incidences and maintains vigilance to ensure security features stay effective against evolving risks.

results.title: Primary 1 Students Enrolled for Tamil as Mother Tongue in Madrasah Irsyad Zuhri Al-Islamiah and Community Outreach Efforts to Encourage Tamil-Speaking Asatizah Development
results.summary: Six Primary 1 students enrolled Tamil as their Mother Tongue Language at Madrasah Irsyad Zuhri Al-Islamiah in 2026. To support admission, a Madrasah Preparatory Programme was introduced at Abdul Gafoor Mosque, led by Indian Muslim community leaders with MUIS support, promoted via Indian Muslim mosques and organisations. Beyond language, religious sector careers require deep knowledge applicable to Singapore's multi-racial context. Tamil-speaking asatizah can access MUIS professional development initiatives, including continuing professional education programmes, to nurture local talent.

results.title: Proportion of Land Area Set Aside for Green Spaces
results.summary: Under the Singapore Green Plan 2030, the government aims to add 1,000 hectares of green spaces by 2035, safeguarded in the URA Master Plan since 2019, while progressing toward planting one million trees by 2030. As of January 2026, over 832,000 trees have been planted through more than 150,000 community participations. The Ministry balances green space expansion with building needs, having already developed 130 hectares of parks in the last five years and safeguarding over 7,800 hectares including nature reserves. To support mental well-being, the Parks for Health Framework identifies 15 contemplative landscape sites and aims to establish 30 therapeutic gardens by 2030 via collaboration with HDB and SingHealth. Heat resilience measures include wind flow considerations in planning and heat stress advisories, with the broader City in Nature vision extending beyond the tree-planting target.

results.title: Proportion of Singapore Citizen Bus Captain Applicants Pre- and Post-PBIMP Launch
results.summary: The Public Bus Industry Manpower Plan launched in 2016 introduced initiatives like improved remuneration and flexible work arrangements to strengthen the local workforce. From 2021 to 2025, the proportion of bus captain job applications from Singapore citizens ranged between 25% and 50%. The Land Transport Authority did not track applicant numbers prior to mid-2020. The Minister noted that comparing citizen application proportions to total applicants is not meaningful, as foreign applications reflect residual manpower needs after local recruitment and are subject to foreign worker quotas.

results.title: Proportion of Social Service Agencies Paying Employees According to Social Service Sector Salary Guidelines
results.summary: Approximately 80% of social service agency employees are currently paid according to the National Council of Social Service (NCSS) Sector Skills and Salary Guidelines. The Ministry for Social and Family Development (MSF) adjusts programme funding to enable agencies to meet these guidelines and engages leaders to benchmark salary adherence against peers. While MSF has not conducted a specific correlation study linking wage non-compliance to staff turnover or vacancy rates, it acknowledges that case load impacts turnover and ensures funding and staffing levels remain commensurate with operational needs. For smaller community-based agencies with limited fundraising capabilities, MSF provides regular engagement, bespoke support, and facilitates access to free corporate pro bono expertise to upgrade human resources practices.

results.title: Proposal to Measure Developments based on Biodiversity Improvement Score
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Landscaping for Urban Spaces and High-Rises (LUSH) programme, which mandates replacement greenery up to 40% of site area, supports biodiversity through a holistic strategy including safeguarding 7,800 hectares of green spaces and restoring habitats. He declined to adopt the UK's Biodiversity Net Gain framework, citing its limitations in the UK context regarding housing delays and costs, and emphasized that Singapore's coordinated approach yields better outcomes than imposing targets on individual projects. While the Minister acknowledged suggestions to complement green plot ratios with ecological indicators or align developments with Ecological Profiling Exercise corridors, he maintained that current measures, such as the Yishun Chencharu project's environmental monitoring, already integrate nature group expertise to enhance urban livability without adopting external models unsuitable for local constraints.

results.title: Public Health Measures and Penalties against Secondhand Smoke Coming from Neighbouring Units and Residential Balconies
results.summary: The Ministry treats secondhand smoke in residential homes as a neighbourly dispute rather than a public health threat, citing the private nature of homes and potential intrusiveness of regulation. While smoking is prohibited in over 49,000 public places including common areas like void decks and stairwells, no penalties are currently imposed for emissions drifting into neighbouring units or smoking on residential balconies. The government employs a community-based approach involving Town Councils and grassroots leaders to encourage social responsibility instead of introducing strict penalties. Policies will be reviewed periodically based on international best practices and public feedback.

results.title: Qualifications and Profiles of Nuclear Energy Office's Full-time Staff
results.summary: The Nuclear Energy Office (NEO) within the Energy Market Authority (EMA) was established to build domestic capabilities for assessing nuclear energy feasibility, requiring diverse expertise beyond nuclear engineering. Among its 26 officers, four hold nuclear engineering degrees while 16 possess science or engineering degrees. All staff undergo specialized training, such as with the International Atomic Energy Agency, and EMA plans to attach officers to overseas counterparts in coming years. As capabilities grow, EMA and other agencies intend to expand their teams and welcome more Singaporeans to join the nuclear energy endeavour.

results.title: Rationale for G1 Humanities Combined Subject Structure and Offering Standalone G1 Humanities Subjects for In-Depth Learning
results.summary: Introduced in 2024 under Full Subject-Based Banding, the G1 Humanities subject combines Social Studies with lighter exposure modules in Geography, History, and Literature to broaden societal understanding and develop global competencies while managing learning loads. This structure ensures all students gain a broad perspective of Singapore society and the world, with inquiry-based methods like field learning used to explore key issues. Students commencing G1 Humanities may transition to specific, more demanding Humanities subjects from Secondary 2 if they demonstrate interest and aptitude. The Ministry of Education will continue reviewing the curriculum to align offerings with diverse student needs.

results.title: Reintroducing Larger-format HDB Flats for Younger Families to Encourage Parenthood and Space Needs Strategies
results.summary: The Minister stated that HDB currently offers 5-room and 3Gen flats with four bedrooms and three bathrooms to cater to larger and multi-generation families. Owners may also apply to combine two adjoining 3-room or smaller flats under the HDB Conversion Scheme. Additionally, the Third Child Priority Scheme provides priority allocation to eligible families with three or more children for up to 5% of flats offered in Build-To-Order and Sale of Balance Flats exercises. The Ministry will continue reviewing housing policies to meet the needs of different groups, including households with more family members.

results.title: Review on Proportion of Singapore's Gold Reserves Stored Overseas
results.summary: Singapore's gold reserves are diversified across geographies to ensure accessibility and resilience against geopolitical and economic shocks. For security reasons, the specific proportion of gold stored outside Singapore is not disclosed. The Government regularly reviews its portfolio to maintain appropriate diversification during periods of economic and geopolitical uncertainty. No specific timeline for a review of the overseas proportion was stated.

results.title: Reviewing Working Mother's Child Relief Scheme and Enhancing Relief Quantum to Offset Rising Costs
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR) remains a fixed dollar amount rather than being pegged to income, as linking relief to earnings would disproportionately benefit higher-income earners and reduce the scheme's progressiveness. While the Government does not plan to increase the WMCR quantum or revert to an income-based calculation, it has enhanced other family supports including the Baby Bonus Cash Gift, Child Development Account, and Large Families Scheme. Recent Budget measures also raised monthly household income thresholds for preschool subsidies and Student Care Fee Assistance to help more families. The Government maintains a commitment to regularly reviewing policies to address parents' needs through comprehensive support rather than relying on a single relief measure.

results.title: Standardised Diagnosis and Care Pathway for Elderly Residents with Possible Hearing Loss
results.summary: Established care pathways already exist for elderly residents with possible hearing loss, eliminating the need for a new standardised framework. Under Project Silver Screen, seniors undergo screening at active ageing and community centres, with referrals to Community Audiology Clinics for diagnostic assessments and hearing aid fitting. Those with complex conditions are referred to public healthcare specialists. Subsidies are available through the Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund, offering up to 90% means-tested support for hearing aids, while severe cases like cochlear implants access MediSave and MediShield Life. Polyclinic and GP attendees also receive assessment and referral for follow-up at designated clinics. No specific timeline for new measures was stated as existing processes are in place.

results.title: Strengthening Resources for Peer Support of Mental Health in Community Settings and Workplaces
results.summary: The Health Promotion Board (HPB) offers peer support training for higher learning institutes, workplaces, and community groups, with hybrid formats and additional modules available from this year. The National Council of Social Service (NCSS) runs a specialist programme for persons with lived experiences, while the Ministry of Manpower and Workplace Safety and Health Council established the Well-being Champions Network to train workplace-appointed Champions. The Agency for Integrated Care provides online awareness modules for frontliners. These peer support structures function as informal scaffolds to guide individuals toward formal care via platforms like MindSG.gov.sg and mindline.sg, rather than replacing professional services. The Ministry does not plan to integrate peer supporters into the formal tiered care model but focuses on increasing education and deploying community care teams to reach harder-to-access groups.

results.title: Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure for the Financial Year 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026
results.summary: Parliament has agreed to the Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure for the financial year 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026, as resolved by the Committee of Supply. The motion was moved by Ms Indranee Rajah to approve the Committee's resolutions regarding the supplementary budget. No specific expenditure figures, programme details, or policy changes were detailed in the provided transcript excerpt. The summary reflects the procedural approval of the supplementary estimates without additional operational constraints or financial data.

results.title: Transport Subsidies or Rebates as Measure to Encourage Marriage and Parenthood
results.summary: The Government provides financial support to help families with young children manage child-raising costs, including transport expenses. All families received $500 in Child LifeSG Credits (CLC) for each Singaporean child aged 12 and below last year and will receive another round this year. Under the Large Families Scheme, eligible families with three or more children receive up to $6,000 in Large Family LifeSG Credits (LFLC) for each third and subsequent child. Both CLC and LFLC can be used to defray the cost of taxi and private hire transport services. The Large Families Scheme also offers special privileges at partner merchants, such as deals when booking car-sharing services. The Government will continue to review policies to strengthen support for families.

results.title: Updated School Guidelines on Adverse Climate Events to Safeguard Student Safety and Well-being
results.summary: The Ministry of Education collaborates with the Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment, the National Environment Agency, and the Ministry of Health to develop integrated guidelines for managing adverse climate events. These measures enable learning to continue with minimal disruption while ensuring student safety, such as modifying or suspending physical activities when risks arise. Guidelines are regularly updated and communicated to schools, with timely mitigation reminders issued before anticipated adverse periods. Parents are kept informed of implemented measures to maintain transparency and support.

results.title: Use of AI Chatbots for Counselling and Mental Health Support by Teenagers and Young Adults
results.summary: The Ministry states that tracking AI chatbot usage for counselling is no longer practical, and generative AI is inappropriate as a replacement for qualified mental health providers due to risks of misinformation and harm. Instead, the government directs users to legitimate, anonymous First Stop services like national mindline 1771 and mindline.sg, which offer 24/7 support via rule-based digital therapeutic exercises validated by clinicians. Safeguards include age assurance measures for app stores by 31 March under the Code of Practice for Online Safety and compliance with the Model AI Governance Framework. While informed consent is not feasible for anonymous services, data privacy is protected through anonymity. The Ministry will continue to evaluate the impact of these interventions and improve resources over time.
